title: Gen2 Energy Secures 195-MW Grid Capacity for Hydrogen Project in Norway
content: Gen2 Energy has confirmed a total capacity reservation of 195 MW for its green hydrogen project at Nesbruket in Vefsn municipality, Norway. Of this total, 87 MW is allocated within the existing power grid, enabling the immediate development of the project's first phase. This initial phase will produce up to 30 tonnes of liquefied green hydrogen per day, specifically targeting the maritime sector to aid in decarbonization. The remaining capacity is contingent upon future grid development in the Helgeland region. CEO Lena Halvari stated that this reservation provides the necessary predictability to move toward a final investment decision and construction. The project is expected to scale up to its full 195 MW capacity over time, fostering local value creation and jobs. Gen2 Energy aims to establish a complete hydrogen value chain to support Norwayâ€™s and the EUâ€™s climate goals.
author: Analysts Account
